notes: Daily draft 2026-06-23. 14 sourced clues. Tier focus: $800 (×3) and $1000 (×1) to address the zero-$1000 gap. Covers Film, Television, Brands & Advertising, Celebrities & Public Figures, Comics & Graphic Novels, and Recent Events.

questions.clue: Arriving 36 years after its 1986 original, this 2022 sequel about a daredevil Navy fighter pilot became the highest-grossing film of its star's career, clearing over $1.5 billion at the worldwide box office and receiving an Academy Award nomination for Best Picture.

questions.clue: In this 1997 thriller, a parolee who claims innocence boards a federal prison transport plane to fly home, only to find the dangerous inmates staging a takeover led by a villain who calls himself 'Cyrus the Virus.'

questions.clue: Samuel L. Jackson plays an FBI agent escorting a witness aboard a Boeing 747 in this 2006 action thriller, in which a cargo of dangerous creatures is released from the hold in an attempt to kill the witness before he can testify.

questions.clue: This Irish actor portrayed the world's most famous fictional spy in four films from 1995 to 2002, then starred in a 2008 ABBA-inspired musical film set on a Greek island opposite Meryl Streep.

questions.clue: An ordained Presbyterian minister trained as a musician at Rollins College, he began his broadcasting career at NBC before spending three decades on public television hosting a preschool program that reassured its young viewers they were liked 'just the way you are.'

questions.clue: Film critic Roger Ebert called this actor 'the most unsung leading man of his generation' in 1992; his career included portrayals of Jim Morrison, Doc Holliday, and a caped Gotham crime-fighter, with his films collectively grossing over $3.85 billion worldwide.

questions.clue: Harvey Kurtzman and William Gaines launched this EC Comics publication in 1952, reformatting it from a comic-book series into a standalone newsstand title; it is the last surviving EC publication and is identified by its grinning, wide-eyed cover mascot.

questions.clue: A Laurence Olivier Award for a West End stage revival and two BAFTAs attest to this Irish actor's range; his Academy Award nomination came for playing a troubled father spending a holiday with his young daughter in a quietly devastating 2022 feature film.

citations.title: Paul Mescal
citations.snippet: Paul Colm Michael Mescal is an Irish actor. His accolades include two BAFTA Awards and a Laurence Olivier Award, in addition to nominations for an Academy Award, a Primetime Emmy Award, and a Golden Globe Award.

questions.clue: Her career began as a teenager in the 2008 Broadway musical '13' before pop stardom brought forty Guinness World Records, and her fragrance business generated over one billion dollars in global retail sales.
